data.content:
I can see one test will fail — `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. Let me start it up for them. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I can see one test that will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express".

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — **expected 15.00, got 10.00**

The watcher is active — every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will re-run automatically. To fix the failing test, change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` in the `CalculateShipping` method (line 23). Want me to fix it?
